body.title: agent_datacenter: migrate lab/claudecode/channel.py to comms://Shared IMAP mailbox
body.result: channel.py: dual-write shim added. post() mirrors to comms://Shared via IMAP Router. read/listen/sessions remain JSONL-backed. JSONL is authoritative until Phase 5 removal. Lazy IMAP init; silent fallback if unavailable.
body.description:
Migrate the current TheIgors channel (lab/claudecode/channel.py, JSONL file-based) to the IMAP-backed Shared mailbox. One-release deprecation shim keeps callers working during migration.

Current state: channel.py reads/writes ~/.TheIgors/cc_channel/messages.jsonl. 307 lines. Used by CC and Igor for shared message passing.

Proposed shape:
- Deprecation shim: channel.py API (read, write) updated to call Router.send('comms://Shared') and imap_server.fetch_unseen('Shared') respectively
- Existing callers (cc_bridge.py, igor's channel reads) work unchanged via the shim
- Old JSONL file: keep for one release as backup; after verification, remove
- Verification: send a message via old channel API; confirm it appears in Shared IMAP mailbox
- Migration complete: remove shim, update callers to call comms:// directly

Affected files:
- ~/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/channel.py (rewritten to call comms:// + IMAP)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_bridge.py (if it calls channel.py; verify and update)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: deprecation comment at top of channel.py: 'DEPRECATED: use comms://Shared directly. Shim retained for one release.'

Scope boundary:
IN: channel.py shim rewrite, JSONL backup retained, caller verification
OUT: removal of JSONL file (that's a separate cleanup after verification)

Test plan:
- channel.write('test message') → imap_server.unseen_count('Shared') == 1
- channel.read() returns the message that was sent
- All existing callers of channel.py work without code changes (shim compatibility)
